caption.full:
It's Always Sunny in Philadelphia is the longest-running live-action.

FX has set August 17 for the Season 18 premiere of It's Always Sunny in Philadelphia. The new season will feature an expanded 10-episode run. The series continues its record-breaking run as the longest-running live-action sitcom in American television history.

Send this to the biggest Always Sunny fan you know!

AI-assisted recap by The Screen Report.

#MovieNews #Hollywood #ItsAlwaysSunny #CharlieDay
caption.firstComment: The gang’s finally stretching to 10 episodes—Season 18 starts Aug 17. Cast your vote for the show’s peak year and tell us the unforgettable moment that sealed it for you.

facebook.text: It's Always Sunny in Philadelphia returns August 17 with a 10-episode Season 18. The series is now the longest-running live-action sitcom in American TV history. The premiere airs at 9 p.m. ET on FXX, FX, and Hulu. What are you most excited to see this season?

youtube.title: Charlie Day: It's Always Sunny Season 18 Aug 17
youtube.description:
Charlie Day stars in It's Always Sunny in Philadelphia Season 18, premiering August 17 on FXX, FX, and Hulu. The expanded 10-episode season cements its status as the longest-running live-action sitcom in American TV history. Glenn Howerton and Danny DeVito also return.

AI-assisted recap by The Screen Report.
